 {"id":"bd-1eak","title":"Add bd refile command to move issues between rigs","description":"Add a `bd refile <source-id> <target-rig>` command to move issues between rigs.

Beads already has the routing infrastructure:
- ResolveBeadsDirForID - routes reads by prefix
- ResolveBeadsDirForRig - routes writes by rig name
- GetRoutedStorageForID - opens cross-rig storage connections

Implementation:
1. Get source issue via routing
2. Create new issue in target rig (existing --prefix mechanism)
3. Close source issue with 'Refiled to <new-id>'
4. Optionally: migrate deps, link the two issues

This is pure beads - no gastown involvement needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-31T12:29:18.161088-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:34:23.548938-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:34:23.548938-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented bd refile command"}

Considerations:
- Should this be in core or a separate binary (bd-tui)?
- What functionality would benefit from a TUI? (list view, issue details, search, bulk operations)

- Dependency cost vs user experience tradeoff
- Target audience: humans who want interactive workflows vs CLI/scripting users

 {"id":"bd-1tkd","title":"Code smell: ComputeContentHash() is 100+ lines of repetitive code","description":"attached_args: Refactor ComputeContentHash repetitive code

The ComputeContentHash() method in internal/types/types.go (lines 87-190) is over 100 lines of repetitive code:

```go
h.Write([]byte(i.Title))
h.Write([]byte{0}) // separator
h.Write([]byte(i.Description))
h.Write([]byte{0})
h.Write([]byte(i.Design))
// ... repeated 40+ times
```

Consider:
1. Using reflection to iterate over struct fields tagged for hashing
2. Creating a helper function that takes field name and value
3. Using a slice of fields to hash and iterating over it
4. At minimum, extracting the repetitive pattern into a helper

 {"id":"bd-2vh3.3","title":"Tier 2: Basic bd mol squash command","description":"Add bd mol squash command for basic molecule execution compression.

## Command

bd mol squash <molecule-id> [flags]
  --dry-run         Preview what would be squashed
  --keep-children   Don't delete ephemeral children after squash
  --json            JSON output

## Implementation

1. Find all ephemeral children of molecule (parent-child deps)
2. Concatenate child descriptions/notes into digest
3. Create digest issue in main repo with:
   - Title: 'Molecule Execution Summary: <original-title>'
   - digest_of: [list of squashed child IDs]
   - ephemeral: false (digest is permanent)
4. Delete ephemeral children (unless --keep-children)
5. Link digest to parent work item

## Schema Changes

Add to Issue struct:
- SquashedAt *time.Time
- SquashDigest string (ID of digest)
- DigestOf []string (IDs of squashed children)

## Acceptance Criteria

- bd mol squash <id> creates digest, removes children
- --dry-run shows preview
- Digest has proper metadata linking","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338114-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T13:53:58.974433-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T13:53:58.974433-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:57:48.338636-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.601321-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.4","title":"Tier 3: AI-powered squash summarization","description":"## Design: Agent-Provided Summarization (Inversion of Control)

bd is a tool FOR agents, not an agent itself. The calling agent provides
the summary; bd just stores it.

### API

```bash
# Agent generates summary, passes to bd
bd mol squash bd-xxx --summary "Agent-generated summary here"

# Without --summary, falls back to basic concatenation
bd mol squash bd-xxx
```

### Gas Town Integration Pattern

```go
// In polecat completion handler or witness
raw := exec.Command("bd", "mol", "show", molID, "--json").Output()
summary := callHaiku(buildSummaryPrompt(raw))  // agent's job
exec.Command("bd", "mol", "squash", molID, "--summary", summary).Run()
```

### Why This Design

| Concern | bd's job | Agent's job |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| Store data | ✅ | |
| Query data | ✅ | |
| Generate summaries | | ✅ |
| Call LLMs | | ✅ |
| Manage API keys | | ✅ |

### Implementation Status

- [x] --summary flag added to bd mol squash
- [x] Tests for agent-provided summary
- [ ] Gas Town integration (separate task)

### Acceptance Criteria

- ✅ bd mol squash --summary uses provided text
- ✅ Without --summary, falls back to concatenation
- ✅ No LLM calls in bd itself","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.732749-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:29:16.288713-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:00.733264-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.698686-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vh3.5","title":"Tier 4: Auto-squash on molecule completion","description":"Automatically squash molecules when they reach terminal state.

## Integration Points

1. Hook into molecule completion handler
2. Detect when all steps are done/failed
3. Trigger squash automatically

## Config

bd config set mol.auto_squash true  # Default: false
bd config set mol.auto_squash_on_success true  # Only on success
bd config set mol.auto_squash_delay '5m'  # Wait before squash

## Implementation Options

### Option A: Post-Completion Hook
In mol completion handler:
- Check if auto_squash enabled
- Call Squash() after terminal state

### Option B: Git Hook
In .beads/hooks/post-commit:
- bd mol squash --auto

### Option C: Daemon Background Task
- Daemon periodically checks for squashable molecules
- Squashes in background

## Acceptance Criteria

- Completed molecules auto-squash without manual intervention
- Configurable delay before squash
- Option to squash only on success vs always
- Works with both daemon and no-daemon modes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.345577-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:40:39.794527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:13.346152-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:22.797141-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2vh3.6","title":"Tier 5 (Future): JSONL archive rotation","description":"Periodic rotation of issues.jsonl for long-running repos.

## Design

.beads/
├── issues.jsonl           # Current (hot)
├── archive/
│   ├── issues-2025-12.jsonl.gz  # Archived (cold)
│   └── ...
└── index.jsonl            # Merged index for queries

## Commands

bd archive rotate [flags]
  --older-than N    Archive issues closed > N days
  --compress        Gzip archives
  --dry-run         Preview

bd archive list           # Show archived periods
bd archive restore <period>  # Restore from archive

## Config

bd config set archive.enabled true
bd config set archive.rotate_days 90
bd config set archive.compress true
bd config set archive.path '.beads/archive'

## Considerations

- Archives can be gitignored (local only) or committed (shared)
- Query layer must check index, hydrate from archive
- Cold storage tiering (S3/GCS) for enterprise
- Merkle proofs preserved for audit

## Priority

This is post-1.0 work. Current focus is on squash (removes ephemeral).
Archive helps with long-term history but is less critical.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210008-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:27:02.371921-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vh3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vh3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:58:38.210543-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-313v","title":"rpc: Rich mutation events not emitted","description":"The activity command (activity.go) references rich mutation event types (MutationBonded, MutationSquashed, MutationBurned, MutationStatus) that include metadata like OldStatus, NewStatus, ParentID, and StepCount.

However, the emitMutation() function in server_core.go:141 only accepts (eventType, issueID) and only populates Type, IssueID, and Timestamp. The additional metadata fields are never set.

Need to either:
1. Add an emitRichMutation() function that accepts the additional metadata
2. Update call sites (close, bond, squash, burn operations) to emit rich events

Without this fix, the activity feed will never show:
- Status transitions (in_progress -> closed)
- Bonded events with step counts
- Parent molecule relationships

Discovered during code review of bd-xo1o implementation.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T04:06:17.39523-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:13:19.205249-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-66l4","title":"Runtime bonding: bd mol attach","description":"Attach a molecule to an already-running workflow.

COMMAND: bd mol attach <epic-id> <proto> [--after <issue-id>]

BEHAVIOR:
- Resolve running epic and proto
- Spawn proto as new subtree
- Wire to specified attachment point (or epic root)
- Handle in-progress issues: new work doesn't block completed work

USE CASES:
- Discovered need for docs while implementing feature
- Hotfix needs attaching to release workflow
- Additional testing scope identified mid-flight

FLAGS:
- --after ISSUE: Specific attachment point within epic
- --type: sequential (default) or parallel
- --var: Variables for the attached proto

CONSIDERATIONS:
- What if epic is already closed? Error or reopen?
- What if attachment point issue is closed? Attach as ready-to-work?","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:16.920483-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T01:08:43.530597-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o5xe","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.435542-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-66l4","depends_on_id":"bd-o91r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T00:59:51.813782-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-687g","title":"Code review: mol squash deletion bypasses tombstone system","description":"The deleteEphemeralChildren function in mol_squash.go uses DeleteIssue directly instead of the proper deletion flow. This bypasses tombstone creation, deletion tracking (deletions.jsonl), and dependency cleanup. Could cause issues with deletion propagation across clones.

Current code uses d.DeleteIssue(ctx, id) but should probably use d.DeleteIssues(ctx, ids, false, true, false) for proper tombstone handling.

Alternative: Document that ephemeral issues intentionally use hard delete since they are transient and should never propagate to other clones anyway.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:57:20.223345-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T14:17:38.073899-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T14:17:38.073899-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-687v","title":"Consider caching external dep resolution results","description":"Each call to GetReadyWork re-checks all external dependencies by:
1. Querying for external deps in the local database
2. Opening each external project's database
3. Querying for closed issues with provides: labels

For workloads with many external deps or slow external databases, this adds latency on every bd ready call.

Potential optimizations:
- In-memory TTL cache for external dep status (e.g., 60 second TTL)
- Store resolved status in a local cache table with timestamp
- Batch resolution of common project/capability pairs

This is not urgent - current implementation is correct and performant for typical workloads. Only becomes an issue with many external deps across many projects.","status":"deferred","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.360877-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:27:02.223409-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-687v","depends_on_id":"bd-zmmy","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-21T23:45:16.361493-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-68e4","title":"doctor --fix should export when DB has more issues than JSONL","description":"When 'bd doctor' detects a count mismatch (DB has more issues than JSONL), it currently recommends 'bd sync --import-only', which imports JSONL into DB. But JSONL is the source of truth, not the DB.

**Current behavior:**
- Doctor detects: DB has 355 issues, JSONL has 292
- Recommends: 'bd sync --import-only' 
- User runs it: Returns '0 created, 0 updated' (no-op, because JSONL hasn't changed)
- User is stuck

**Root cause:**
The doctor fix is one-directional (JSONL→DB) when it should be bidirectional. If DB has MORE issues, they haven't been exported yet - the fix should be 'bd export' (DB→JSONL), not import.

**Desired fix:**
In fix.DBJSONLSync(), detect which has more data:
- If DB > JSONL: Run 'bd export' to sync JSONL (since DB is the working copy)
- If JSONL > DB: Run 'bd sync --import-only' to import (JSONL is source of truth)
- If equal but timestamps differ: Detect based on file mtime

This makes 'bd doctor --fix' actually fix the problem instead of being a no-op.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-21T11:17:20.994319182-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T11:23:24.38523731-07:00"}
 {"id":"bd-6a5z","title":"Add stale molecule check to bd doctor","description":"Extend bd doctor to detect stale molecules.

**New check:**
- Name: 'Stale Molecules'
- Category: Workflow
- Severity: Warning (don't fail overall check)

**Detection:**
Reuse logic from bd mol stale command:
- Find mols where Completed >= Total but root is open
- Filter to orphaned (not assigned, not pinned)
- Extra weight if blocking other work

**Output:**
```
⚠ Stale Molecules
  Found 2 complete-but-unclosed molecules:
  - bd-xyz: Version bump v0.36.0 (blocking 1 issue)
  - bd-uvw: Old patrol (not blocking)
  Fix: bd close <id> or bd mol squash <id>
```

**--fix behavior:**
- Auto-close stale mols (with reason 'Auto-closed by bd doctor')
- Or prompt interactively with -i flag

Depends on: bd mol stale command","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T18:23:24.549941-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T12:42:50.288442-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T12:42:50.288442-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6a5z","depends_on_id":"bd-anv2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T18:23:48.682552-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-is6m","title":"Add gate checking to Deacon patrol loop","description":"Integrate gate checking into Deacon's patrol cycle.

## Patrol Integration
```go
func (d *Deacon) checkGates(ctx context.Context) {
    gates, _ := d.store.ListOpenGates(ctx)
    
    for _, gate := range gates {
        // Check timeout
        if time.Since(gate.CreatedAt) > gate.Timeout {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "timeout")
            d.closeGate(gate, "timed out")
            continue
        }
        
        // Check condition
        if d.checkCondition(gate.AwaitType, gate.AwaitID) {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "cleared")
            d.closeGate(gate, "condition met")
        }
    }
}
```

## Note
This task is in Gas Town (gt), not beads.  {"id":"bd-iz5t","title":"Swarm: 13 beads backlog issues for polecat execution","description":"## Swarm Overview

13 issues prepared for parallel polecat execution. All issues have been enhanced with concrete implementation guidance, file lists, and success criteria.

| bd-ykd9 | Doctor --fix auto-repair |

### Polish (2)

|----|------|

| bd-u2sc.4 | slog structured logging |

## Issue Details

-{"id":"bd-m964","title":"Consider FTS5 for text search at scale","description":"SearchIssues uses LIKE patterns for text search which can't use indexes.

**Current query (queries.go:1475-1477):**
```sql
(title LIKE ? OR description LIKE ? OR id LIKE ?)
```

**Problem:** Full table scan on every text search. At 100K+ issues, this becomes slow.

**SQLite FTS5 solution:**
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
  id, title, description, design, notes,
  content='issues',
  content_rowid='rowid'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
  INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description, design, notes)
  VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description, new.design, new.notes);
END;
-- (similar for UPDATE, DELETE)
```

**Trade-offs:**
- Database size increase (~30-50% for text content)
- Additional write overhead (trigger execution)
- Better search capabilities (ranking, phrase search)

**Decision needed:** Is full-text search a priority feature? Current LIKE search may be acceptable for most use cases.

**Benchmark first:** Measure SearchIssues at 100K scale before implementing.","status":"deferred","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466121-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T21:14:21.937564-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m964","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:56.466764-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-mol-0qm","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"attached_args: Release v0.38.0 - execute each step in order, verify before proceeding to next

Release checklist for beads version 0.38.0.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: 0.38.0 - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for 0.38.0.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "0.38.0",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [0.38.0] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v0.38.0"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v0.38.0
git push origin v0.38.0
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v0.38.0

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows 0.38.0
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh 0.38.0 --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.
Needs: wait-for-ci","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"molecule","created_at":"2025-12-27T00:29:46.694495-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-28T01:24:39.173644-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-28T01:24:39.173644-08:00","deleted_by":"daemon","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"molecule"}

-{"id":"bd-zw72","title":"Investigate incremental blocked_issues_cache updates at scale","description":"Current blocked_issues_cache strategy does full DELETE + INSERT on every dependency/status change.

**Problem at scale:**
- 10K issues: ~50ms rebuild (acceptable)
- 100K issues: ~500ms rebuild (noticeable)
- 1M issues: multi-second rebuilds (problematic)

**Current implementation (blocked_cache.go:104-154):**
- DELETE FROM blocked_issues_cache
- INSERT with recursive CTE

**Potential optimizations:**
1. **Incremental updates:** Only add/remove affected issue IDs instead of full rebuild
2. **Dirty tracking:** Skip rebuild if cache is already valid
3. **Async rebuild:** Rebuild in background, serve stale cache briefly
4. **Partial invalidation:** Only invalidate affected subtree

+{"id":"bd-zw72","title":"Investigate incremental blocked_issues_cache updates at scale","description":"Current blocked_issues_cache strategy does full DELETE + INSERT on every dependency/status change.

**Problem at scale:**
- 10K issues: ~50ms rebuild (acceptable)
- 100K issues: ~500ms rebuild (noticeable)
- 1M issues: multi-second rebuilds (problematic)

**Current implementation (blocked_cache.go:104-154):**
- DELETE FROM blocked_issues_cache
- INSERT with recursive CTE

**Potential optimizations:**
1. **Incremental updates:** Only add/remove affected issue IDs instead of full rebuild
2. **Dirty tracking:** Skip rebuild if cache is already valid
3. **Async rebuild:** Rebuild in background, serve stale cache briefly
4. **Partial invalidation:** Only invalidate affected subtree

**Decision needed:** Is this premature optimization? Current target is <100K issues.

**Benchmark:** Add benchmark for cache rebuild at 100K scale to measure actual impact.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.165718-08:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T00:17:14.280698-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zw72","depends_on_id":"bd-h0we","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2025-12-22T22:58:55.166427-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-zy3z","title":"Support wisps in nodb mode for lightweight Gas Town rigs","description":"Enable proper wisp support in --no-db mode to allow lightweight Gas Town rigs (including HQ/Mayor) to run without SQLite overhead.

## Motivation
Gas Town HQ and simple rigs could benefit from nodb mode's simplicity. Currently wisps don't work properly in nodb mode.

## Proposed Design

### Phase 1: Session-scoped wisps
- Memory storage already has Wisp field on Issue
- Wisps exist only for the session lifetime
- Acceptable for patrol cycles that complete within a session
- Depends on: bd-9avq (fix the leak)

### Phase 2: Optional wisp persistence
- Add `.beads/wisps.jsonl` for wisp storage (gitignored)
- Load wisps from this file on nodb startup
- Save wisps to this file on nodb exit
- Wisps persist across restarts but don't sync via git

### Phase 3: Verify mol commands
- Test mol squash, burn, bond work with memory storage
- These currently check for SQLite storage type
- May need interface-based approach instead of type assertions

## Files affected
- cmd/bd/nodb.go - wisp filtering and optional persistence
- internal/storage/memory/ - verify wisp field handling
- cmd/bd/mol_*.go - verify memory storage compatibility
- .beads/.gitignore - add wisps.jsonl","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T21:15:59.658799-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T21:17:11.570958-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T21:17:11.570958-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zy3z","depends_on_id":"bd-9avq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-24T21:16:04.649866-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
